spec: SPEC §4c — REAL cross-engine value-net check (not the ulp proxy)
method: cp_erf/cp_gelu/cp_value copied verbatim from js/cheap_planner.js; run in both engines (dev/exact_nn/verifier/ce_engine_eval.js) on identical harvested plan states; 16 vprior scores compared as raw float64 bit patterns.
finding: Prod's value-net forward is BIT-IDENTICAL between V8-20 and SpiderMonkey-115 on the realized plan-state distribution — both use fdlibm-derived exp/pow. A V8-calibrated τ transfers exactly to SpiderMonkey (not merely within the ~1e-15 ulp-proxy headroom).
caveats: JSC (Safari) not yet measured — its exp/pow lineage differs; check before any Safari T2 claim.
Measures the value-net path (Math.exp via erf); the E3D Math.pow cand0-coordinate path expected identical by the same fdlibm mechanism but not separately measured.
5459 calibration-seed states, not the full input domain; the ulp proxy remains the conservative worst-case bound (0 flips there too).
